python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
path = "crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs"
s = open(path).read()
s = s.replace(
    "Ok(Array::from_iter(graphemes.into_iter().map(Str::from))\n                    .into_value())",
    "Ok(Array::from_iter(graphemes.into_iter().map(|s| Value::Str(Str::from(s))))\n                    .into_value())"
)
open(path, "w").write(s)
EOF

content.text: I'll use `.as_str().contains(c)` to call the inherent `str::contains` instead of `Str::contains`.

content.text: All behaviors work as expected: normal extraction, count-based arrays, unicode, strip, and error cases. Now let's write the final diff.

input.command: git status --short && git diff -- crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs > patch.txt && cat patch.txt

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`, contains only the intended source change to `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`, and compiles/runs correctly (verified via `cargo check` and manual smoke tests of `count`, `strip`, unicode, and error cases).

Summary: implemented both features by adding `count: Option<usize>` and `strip: Option<Str>` named parameters to `Str::first`/`Str::last`. Without `count`, behavior is unchanged (single grapheme `Str`); with `count`, an `Array` of grapheme-cluster `Str`s is returned, erroring via a new `count_exceeds_length` message if the (post-strip) string has fewer graphemes than requested. `strip` trims matching leading/trailing characters before extraction, reusing the existing `string_is_empty` error if trimming empties the string.

result:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`, contains only the intended source change to `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`, and compiles/runs correctly (verified via `cargo check` and manual smoke tests of `count`, `strip`, unicode, and error cases).

Summary: implemented both features by adding `count: Option<usize>` and `strip: Option<Str>` named parameters to `Str::first`/`Str::last`. Without `count`, behavior is unchanged (single grapheme `Str`); with `count`, an `Array` of grapheme-cluster `Str`s is returned, erroring via a new `count_exceeds_length` message if the (post-strip) string has fewer graphemes than requested. `strip` trims matching leading/trailing characters before extraction, reusing the existing `string_is_empty` error if trimming empties the string.
